page: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 96 physical cores of the ARM64-based AWS Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating at 2.8 GHz with a dedicated CPU allocation, the system features 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 36 MB L3 cache and supports AES cryptography, though it lacks AVX and AMX instruction sets. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. It achieves a stress-ng multi-core score of 163,907.12 and a PassMark memory score of 3,409.51, with uncached read memory bandwidth reaching 481.15 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the top decile, with Redis handling 198.34 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench image processing scoring in the average tier.

The lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs limits this server's utility for local-disk-heavy databases or deep learning training workloads. Additionally, while text generation for medium and large language models is strong, text generation for small models falls into the bottom decile. Given its high memory density, high memory bandwidth, and strong multi-core performance, the r8g.metal-24xl is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
description: The Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 physical ARM64 cores on the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, which represents a tradeoff for storage-heavy or acceleration-dependent tasks. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and highly efficient database and Redis operations. Conversely, single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are average to poor. This configuration is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, including large in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and enterprise applications requiring massive memory capacity without local disk dependencies.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 ARM64 cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 ARM64 cores and 768 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized ARM64 server delivering high-density DDR5 memory and top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise database workloads.

Equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB per core.
